skill.name: prometheus-configuration
skill.description: Set up Prometheus for comprehensive metric collection, storage, and monitoring of infrastructure and applications. Use when implementing metrics collection, setting up monitoring infrastructure, or configuring alerting rules.
skill.summary: Configure Prometheus for comprehensive metric collection, alerting, and monitoring of infrastructure and applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate Prometheus configuration documentation skill. The static scanner flagged code examples within markdown documentation as executable code, which is a false positive. All detected patterns (external commands, network URLs, certificate paths, crypto algorithms) appear in YAML/bash code blocks showing configuration examples - not actual runtime code. No malicious intent detected. Skill is safe for publication.

critical_findings.title: False Positive: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access Heuristic
critical_findings.description: Static scanner flagged dangerous combination heuristic triggered by documentation code examples. This is a documentation skill showing Prometheus config examples in YAML and bash code blocks. No actual code execution occurs.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: Heuristic triggers on documentation patterns. SKILL.md is purely educational content with YAML/bash examples, not executable code.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Detection
medium_findings.description: Backtick and $() patterns appear in bash command examples within documentation. Not actual shell execution - users would run these commands themselves in their terminal.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backtick/$ patterns are in code fences showing example commands users would run. No exec() or system() calls in actual skill code.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Network URLs Detection
medium_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs are legitimate Prometheus infrastructure: Helm chart repository and localhost for testing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to prometheus-community.github.io (official Helm charts) and localhost:9090 (local Prometheus). No exfiltration or malicious endpoints.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Certificate/Key File Detection
medium_findings.description: Example TLS configuration paths in YAML documentation. Not actual sensitive files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shows /etc/prometheus/ca.crt etc as example paths in TLS config documentation. Placeholder paths, not real credentials.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
medium_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly identifies Prometheus terminology as crypto algorithms. 'alert', 'rate', 'expr' are Prometheus config keywords, not crypto.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Prometheus uses 'alert' for alerting rules, 'rate' for rate queries, 'expr' for expressions. No cryptographic algorithms present.

content.value_statement: Set up Prometheus for comprehensive metric collection, alerting, and infrastructure monitoring. This skill provides complete configuration guides for scrape targets, recording rules, and alert definitions.
actual_capabilities: Generate Prometheus server configuration with scrape targets
Create recording rules for pre-computed metrics
Design alert rules with severity levels and annotations
Set up TLS authentication for scrape endpoints
limitations: Does not install Prometheus binaries or containers
Does not provide managed Prometheus hosting
Requires user to apply configurations to their Prometheus instance

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up Monitoring
use_cases.description: Configure Prometheus from scratch for a new infrastructure project with proper scrape targets and alerting

use_cases.title: SRE Team Defining Alert Rules
use_cases.description: Create comprehensive alert rules for service availability, error rates, and resource utilization
use_cases.target_user: Site reliability engineers defining SLOs and alerts

use_cases.title: Developer Instrumenting Applications
use_cases.description: Learn Prometheus metric naming conventions and scrape configuration for application endpoints

prompt_templates.title: Basic Prometheus Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Prometheus monitoring for my Kubernetes cluster. Include scrape configuration for node exporters and a basic alert for service availability.

prompt_templates.title: Recording Rules Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create recording rules for common API metrics including request rate, error rate percentage, and P95 latency. Show how to organize them by service.

prompt_templates.title: Alert Rules Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Design alert rules for high CPU usage (threshold 80% for 5 minutes), high memory usage (threshold 85%), and disk space low (threshold 90%). Include proper labels and annotations.

prompt_templates.title: Kubernetes Service Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure Prometheus to automatically discover services in Kubernetes using annotations. Show relabeling rules to add environment and region labels.

output_examples.input: Set up Prometheus monitoring for my Kubernetes cluster
output:
## Kubernetes Prometheus Setup

### Installation with Helm

```bash
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm install prometheus prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --create-namespace
```

### Basic Scrape Configuration

```yaml
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'kubernetes-nodes'
    kubernetes_sd_configs:
      - role: node
```

output_examples.input: Create recording rules for API metrics
output:
## Recording Rules for API

```yaml
groups:
  - name: api_metrics
    rules:
      - record: job:http_requests:rate5m
        expr: sum by (job) (rate(http_requests_total[5m]))
      
      - record: job:http_requests_error_rate:percentage
        expr: |-
          (sum by (job) (rate(http_requests_total{status=~"5.."}[5m])) 
           / job:http_requests:rate5m) * 100
```

## Alert Rule: High CPU Usage

```yaml
- alert: HighCPUUsage
  expr: instance:node_cpu:utilization > 80
  for: 5m
  labels:
    severity: warning
  annotations:
    summary: "High CPU usage on {{ $labels.instance }}"
    description: "CPU usage is {{ $value }}%"
```

best_practices: Use consistent metric naming with prefix_unit format (e.g., http_requests_total)
Set appropriate scrape intervals (15-60s typical, 5s for critical metrics)
Create recording rules for frequently queried expensive expressions
anti_patterns: Avoid scraping too frequently (high resource usage)
Do not alert on instantaneous spikes without for duration
Avoid recording rules that duplicate raw metrics

faq.question: What scrape interval should I use?
faq.answer: Default is 15 seconds. Use 15-60s for most metrics. Use 5-10s for critical real-time metrics but this increases storage and compute requirements.

faq.question: How do I secure Prometheus scrape endpoints?
faq.answer: Use TLS configuration in prometheus.yml with ca_file, cert_file, and key_file options. Enable basic auth via authorization headers.

faq.question: What is the difference between recording rules and alerts?
faq.answer: Recording rules pre-compute expensive queries into new metrics for faster dashboard queries. Alerts trigger notifications when conditions are met.

faq.question: How do I discover targets in Kubernetes?
faq.answer: Use kubernetes_sd_configs with roles: pod, service, node, or ingress. Use prometheus.io annotations like prometheus.io/scrape: 'true'.

faq.question: How do I validate my configuration?
faq.answer: Use promtool check config prometheus.yml and promtool check rules rules.yml to validate syntax before reloading Prometheus.

faq.question: What retention period should I use?
faq.answer: Default is 15 days. Adjust based on storage capacity and monitoring requirements. Use Thanos or Cortex for long-term storage beyond 30 days.
